subject: LU-9887 lfsck: calculate LFSCK speed properly

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 1:

Both numbers are 64 bit for the div_u64 calls. This was done to avoid

https://jira.hpdd.intel.com/browse/LU-6174
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Patch Set 1:

div_u64() uses 32-bits divisor also, it just returns 64-bits value.

Patch Set 1:

On the other hand, for LFSCK case, the divisor is the LFSCK run time with second unit. It is impossible that the LFSCK run time exceeds 2^32 seconds, so do_div() is enough for LFSCK.

parents.subject: LU-9933 lnet: Handle ping buffer with only loopback NID

commit.subject: LU-9887 lfsck: calculate LFSCK speed properly
commit.message:
LU-9887 lfsck: calculate LFSCK speed properly

Originally, we used do_div(a,b) to calculate the LFSCK average
speed, and got the result from the parameter @a. But later, we
replaced do_div(a,b) with div_u64(a,b). The latter one doesn't
stores the quotient in the parameter @a, instead, the quotient
is returned via the function return value. The patch fixes the
LFSCK logic to obtain the LFSCK average speed from div_u64(a,b)
return value.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I442fb8f7e6c51a4853ea37694e3c221f97e26b19
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/28617
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
